allow_rule: Exact activation authority closes site activation.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The activation authority AUTH-MIR-063 matches site S-44, protocol P6.0, consent C9, and specifies activation date 2026-06-29 before enrollment. However, the action_date in the packet is 2026-06-28, which is one day before the activation date authorized by the source record. Feasibility note FEAS-MIR-063 correctly states it is not enrollment activation authority, which matches the action boundary. The narrow defect is the action_date does not align with the exact activation date specified in the activation authority record.","cited_artifacts":["AUTH-MIR-063","ACT-MIR-063","FEAS-MIR-063","STD-MIR-063-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: The activation authority AUTH-MIR-063 matches site S-44, protocol P6.0, consent C9, and specifies activation date 2026-06-29 before enrollment. However, the action_date in the packet is 2026-06-28, which is one day before the activation date authorized by the source record. Feasibility note FEAS-MIR-063 correctly states it is not enrollment activation authority, which matches the action boundary. The narrow defect is the action_date does not align with the exact activation date specified in the activation authority record.

allow_rule: Exact activation authority closes site activation.
failure_class_notes: Clone of the v3 042 seam in clinical activation.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"Two narrow source-record defects prevent execution. First, AUTH-MIR-063 states consent C9 activation authority remains pending, contradicting the policy requirement that activation authority must match site, protocol version, and consent version before enrollment. Second, ACT-MIR-063 records activation date as 2026-06-29, which is after the action_date of 2026-06-28, violating the pre-execution timing requirement. Feasibility approval FEAS-MIR-063 explicitly disclaims activation authority.","cited_artifacts":["AUTH-MIR-063","ACT-MIR-063","FEAS-MIR-063","STD-MIR-063-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Two narrow source-record defects prevent execution. First, AUTH-MIR-063 states consent C9 activation authority remains pending, contradicting the policy requirement that activation authority must match site, protocol version, and consent version before enrollment. Second, ACT-MIR-063 records activation date as 2026-06-29, which is after the action_date of 2026-06-28, violating the pre-execution timing requirement. Feasibility approval FEAS-MIR-063 explicitly disclaims activation authority.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The hot-work permit PERMIT-OST-065 has a start date of 2026-06-30, but the action_date is 2026-06-28, meaning the permit is not yet effective at the time the hot-work start is requested. Although all permit fields (contractor, site, work type, fire watch) match exactly, the timing dependency fails because the policy requires the permit to be valid before work begins, and the permit's start date has not yet arrived.
